Title: Enable thumbnail job for favicons
Preconditions: White Labeling module installed; Admin access to backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Admin at ${BACK_URL}
2. Sign in with admin credentials
3. Navigate to Settings > Jobs
4. Locate thumbnail generation job
5. Enable the job
6. Add new run for favicon generation
7. Wait for job completion
8. Navigate to White Labeling settings
9. Verify favicons generated in multiple sizes
Expected Result: Thumbnail job enabled successfully; Favicons generated in required sizes (16x16, 32x32, 180x180, 192x192, 512x512); Favicons accessible via whiteLabelingSettings.favicons array

Title: Store and organization have no whitelabeling settings - default branding displayed
Preconditions: Store exists with White Labeling enabled but no custom settings; Organization exists with no branding configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to Admin > Stores > B2B-store
2. Open White Labeling settings tab
3. Verify Enabled = ON but no custom logo/favicon/theme set (all empty)
4. Navigate to Customer > Organizations > Test Org
5. Open White Labeling settings
6. Verify no custom branding configured (all empty)
7. Sign in to storefront with user from Test Org
8. Navigate to homepage at ${FRONT_URL}
9. Inspect header logo
10. Inspect browser tab favicon
11. Inspect page theme colors
Expected Result: Default platform branding displayed: Default logo in header; Default favicon in browser tab (Virto Commerce icon); Default theme preset colors applied; No organization-specific branding

Title: Store has whitelabeling settings but organization does not - store defaults shown
Preconditions: Store has White Labeling enabled with custom logo/favicon/theme; Organization has White Labeling enabled but no custom settings
Steps:
1. Navigate to Admin > Stores > B2B-store
2. Open White Labeling settings
3. Upload store-level logo: store-logo.png
4. Upload store-level favicon: store-favicon.ico
5. Set theme preset: Coffee
6. Save store settings
7. Navigate to Customer > Organizations > Org Without Branding
8. Open White Labeling settings
9. Verify Enabled = ON but no custom logo/favicon/theme
10. Save organization settings
11. Sign in to storefront with user from Org Without Branding
12. Navigate to homepage
13. Inspect header logo (should show store logo)
14. Inspect favicon (should show store favicon)
15. Inspect theme colors (should be Coffee preset)
Expected Result: Store-level branding displayed when organization has no custom settings: Store logo displayed in header; Store favicon displayed in browser tab; Coffee theme colors applied; No organization-specific overrides

Title: Store and organization both have whitelabeling settings - organization branding overrides store
Preconditions: Store has White Labeling with store-level logo/favicon/theme; Organization has custom logo/favicon/theme configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to Admin > Stores > B2B-store
2. Open White Labeling settings
3. Upload store logo: store-logo.png
4. Set theme: Default
5. Save
6. Navigate to Customer > Organizations > Branded Org
7. Open White Labeling settings
8. Verify Enabled = ON
9. Upload organization logo: org-logo.png
10. Upload organization favicon: org-favicon.ico
11. Set theme preset: Coffee
12. Configure footer links list: org-footer-links
13. Configure main menu links list: org-main-menu
14. Save organization settings
15. Sign in to storefront with user from Branded Org
16. Navigate to homepage
17. Inspect header logo (should show org logo, not store logo)
18. Inspect favicon (should show org favicon)
19. Inspect theme (should be Coffee, not Default)
20. Inspect main menu links structure
21. Inspect footer links structure
Expected Result: Organization branding overrides store-level settings: Organization logo displayed in header (org-logo.png); Organization favicon in browser tab; Coffee theme applied (not Default); Organization-specific main menu links displayed; Organization-specific footer links displayed; Store branding NOT shown

Title: Set non-existent theme preset - default theme applied as fallback
Preconditions: Store/Organization White Labeling configured; Access to backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Admin > Customer > Organizations > Test Org
2. Open White Labeling settings
3. Directly set themePresetName field to non-existent value: Mercury (outdated preset)
4. Save settings
5. Sign in to storefront with user from Test Org
6. Navigate to homepage
7. Inspect page theme colors and styles
8. Verify theme applied
Expected Result: Application handles non-existent preset gracefully: Default theme preset applied as fallback (NOT Mercury); No console errors related to theme loading; Page renders correctly with default colors; User is NOT shown broken/missing styles

Title: Set custom logo and favicon for organization - displayed after sign-in

Steps:
1. Navigate to Admin > Customer > Organizations > Company1
2. Open White Labeling settings
3. Verify Enabled = ON
4. Upload custom logo: company1-logo.png (PNG, 200x60px)
5. Upload custom favicon: company1-favicon.ico (ICO, 32x32px)
6. Save settings
7. Open new browser tab
8. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL}
9. Sign in with user account from Company1 organization
10. After successful sign-in, inspect header
11. Inspect browser tab favicon
12. Take screenshot for documentation
Expected Result: Custom branding displayed for organization user: Company1 logo visible in header (company1-logo.png); Company1 favicon displayed in browser tab; Logo is high quality (not pixelated); Favicon displays correctly in tab; Branding persists across page navigation within storefront

Title: Add parent footer links for organization - displayed in storefront footer

2. Create new link list: footer-org1
3. Add parent links:
   - Title: About Us, URL: /about, Priority: 1
   - Title: Customer Support, URL: /support, Priority: 2
   - Title: Legal, URL: /legal, Priority: 3
4. Save link list
5. Navigate to Customer > Organizations > Organization1
6. Open White Labeling settings
7. Set Footer Link List: footer-org1
8. Save organization settings
9. Sign in to storefront with Organization1 user
10. Scroll to footer
11. Inspect footer links structure
Expected Result: Parent footer links displayed correctly: Three parent sections visible: About Us, Customer Support, Legal; Links displayed in priority order (1, 2, 3); Link titles match configured values; Link URLs are correct; Footer structure is well-formatted

Title: Add children footer links under parent - hierarchical structure displayed

Steps:

2. Expand parent link: Customer Support
3. Add child links:
   - Title: Contact Us, URL: /contact, Priority: 1
   - Title: FAQ, URL: /faq, Priority: 2
   - Title: Returns, URL: /returns, Priority: 3
4. Save link list
5. Refresh storefront (signed in as Organization1 user)
6. Scroll to footer
7. Inspect Customer Support section
8. Verify child links nested under parent
Expected Result: Hierarchical footer structure displayed: Customer Support parent link visible; Three child links nested under Customer Support: Contact Us, FAQ, Returns; Child links indented or visually grouped under parent; Priority order respected (Contact Us first, Returns last); Clicking child links navigates correctly; Other parent sections (About Us, Legal) remain unchanged

Title: Verify main menu links returned in whiteLabelingSettings GraphQL query (VCST-4637)

Title: Verify pageContext query includes whiteLabelingSettings with mainMenuLinks
Preconditions: Organization with full branding configured (logo, favicon, theme, footer, mainMenu); pageContext query available in xFrontend
Expected Result: pageContext returns whiteLabelingSettings with mainMenuLinks: Single pageContext query returns 4 sub-objects: slugInfo, store, whiteLabelingSettings, user; whiteLabelingSettings includes logoUrl, faviconUrl, themePresetName, footerLinks, mainMenuLinks; mainMenuLinks array returned with full hierarchy; Data is consistent with separate whiteLabelingSettings query; Response is fast (~200-400ms); Frontend can use single query instead of 4 separate queries (performance optimization)

Title: Switch organization - verify branding updates dynamically (logo and favicon)
Preconditions: User account is member of 2 organizations: Org A (logo-a.png, favicon-a.ico, Default theme) and Org B (logo-b.png, favicon-b.ico, Coffee theme)
Steps:
1. Sign in to storefront with multi-org user account
2. Verify current organization is Org A
3. Inspect header logo (should be logo-a.png)
4. Inspect browser tab favicon (should be favicon-a.ico)
5. Inspect theme colors (should be Default)
6. Click organization switcher in header
7. Select Org B from dropdown
8. Wait for organization switch to complete
9. Verify page refreshes or updates
10. Inspect header logo (should NOW be logo-b.png)
11. Inspect browser tab favicon (should NOW be favicon-b.ico)
12. Inspect theme colors (should NOW be Coffee)
13. Switch back to Org A
14. Verify logo/favicon/theme revert to Org A branding
Expected Result: Organization switching updates branding dynamically: Switching from Org A to Org B updates header logo from logo-a.png to logo-b.png; Browser tab favicon updates from favicon-a.ico to favicon-b.ico; Theme updates from Default to Coffee (colors change); Branding transition is smooth (no broken images, no flash of wrong branding); Switching back to Org A restores Org A branding; Cart contents may reset (org-specific cart); All branding changes persist across page navigation after switch

Title: Switch organization - verify main menu links update dynamically
Preconditions: User is member of Org A (main menu: Products, Services, Contact) and Org B (main menu: Solutions, Partners, Support, Blog)
Steps:
1. Sign in with multi-org user
2. Verify current org is Org A
3. Inspect main menu links in header: Products, Services, Contact
4. Hover over links to verify dropdowns (if any)

6. Wait for refresh
7. Inspect main menu links in header: Should NOW show Solutions, Partners, Support, Blog

10. Verify main menu reverts to Products, Services, Contact
Expected Result: Organization switch updates main menu links: Org A displays main menu: Products, Services, Contact; Switching to Org B updates main menu to: Solutions, Partners, Support, Blog; Main menu structure reflects new organization's link list; Dropdown hierarchies update correctly (children change based on org); Switching back to Org A restores original main menu; No stale links from previous org displayed; Navigation works correctly after org switch

Title: Switch organization - verify footer links update dynamically

Title: Switch organization - verify theme preset changes dynamically
Preconditions: User is member of Org A (Default theme) and Org B (Coffee theme)

Title: Verify branding transition is smooth during organization switch (no flash of wrong branding)
Preconditions: User is member of 2 organizations with distinct branding; Fast internet connection for accurate performance test
Steps:
1. Sign in with multi-org user (Org A)
2. Open browser DevTools > Network tab
3. Switch organization to Org B
4. Observe page reload/refresh
5. Watch for logo/favicon loading
6. Verify no flash of old branding during transition
7. Measure time for branding to fully update
8. Repeat switch 3 times (Org A ↔ Org B)
Expected Result: Smooth branding transition during org switch: No flash of Org A logo before Org B logo loads; No flash of Org A favicon before Org B favicon loads; Theme colors transition smoothly (no jarring color flash); Branding update completes within 1-2 seconds; Page does not show broken images or layout during transition; Loading states are handled gracefully (spinner or skeleton); User experience is professional and polished

Title: Verify logo has proper alt text for accessibility

Verify alt text is descriptive (e.g., "Company1 Logo" or "B2B-store") 7. Enable screen reader (NVDA, JAWS, or VoiceOver) 8. Tab to logo element 9. Verify screen reader announces logo with alt text

Title: Verify pageContext query performance - single call vs multiple queries
Preconditions: Organization with full branding configured; GraphQL endpoint accessible; Performance testing tool (Postman, Insomnia, or browser DevTools)
Steps:
1. Navigate to GraphQL playground
2. Test 1: Execute 4 separate queries sequentially:
   - slugInfo query (measure time)
   - store query (measure time)
   - whiteLabelingSettings query (measure time)
   - user query (measure time)
   Total time = sum of all 4
3. Test 2: Execute single pageContext query that returns all 4 sub-objects (measure time)
4. Compare total time: Test 1 (4 queries) vs Test 2 (1 query)
5. Repeat test 5 times for average
6. Calculate time savings
Expected Result: pageContext query is faster than 4 separate queries: 4 separate queries take ~800-1200ms total (200-300ms each); Single pageContext query takes ~400-600ms (50-70% faster); Time savings: 300-600ms (significant performance improvement); Single query reduces HTTP overhead (fewer round trips); Frontend benefits from loading branding data in one call; pageContext optimization measurable and significant; Performance improvement is consistent across multiple test runs

Title: Verify storefront page load time with whitelabeling enabled - under 3 seconds

3. Set throttling: Fast 3G (or No throttling for best-case)
4. Clear browser cache

6. Navigate to homepage (measure load time from navigation start to page fully loaded)
7. Measure: DOMContentLoaded, Load, Total time
8. Verify logo, favicon, theme assets load within total time
9. Repeat 3 times for average
Expected Result: Storefront page load with whitelabeling is performant: Total page load time < 3 seconds (with Fast 3G throttling); Logo image loads within 500ms; Favicon loads within 200ms; Theme CSS loads within 300ms; DOMContentLoaded < 1.5 seconds; Full page load (including images) < 3 seconds; Whitelabeling assets (logo, favicon, theme CSS) do not significantly slow page load; Page is usable within 2 seconds (Time to Interactive); Meets acceptable performance standards

Title: Verify logo image loading with lazy loading or optimization

3. Filter: Images

Expected Result: Logo image is optimized: Logo image format is appropriate (PNG, SVG, or WebP preferred); Logo file size is reasonable (< 100KB, ideally < 50KB); Logo loading is NOT blocking page render; Logo appears quickly after page load; If logo is large, lazy loading or progressive loading is used; Logo image is compressed (not original unoptimized upload); CDN or caching is used for logo (if applicable); Logo does not cause performance issues

Title: Verify favicon caching to avoid repeated requests
Preconditions: Organization with custom favicon; Browser cache enabled; Chrome DevTools Network tab
Steps:
1. Sign in to storefront with branded org user
2. Navigate to homepage (clear cache first)
3. Open DevTools > Network tab
4. Observe favicon request (favicon.ico or .png)
5. Verify response headers (Cache-Control, ETag)
6. Navigate to another page (catalog)
7. Observe Network tab - verify favicon is NOT requested again (served from cache)
8. Close browser, reopen, navigate to storefront
9. Verify favicon is served from disk cache (not re-downloaded)
Expected Result: Favicon is properly cached: First request for favicon returns HTTP 200 with Cache-Control header; Subsequent page navigations do NOT request favicon again (served from memory cache); After browser restart, favicon is served from disk cache (HTTP 304 or disk cache hit); Favicon is NOT re-downloaded on every page load; Caching strategy reduces network overhead; Cache headers are configured correctly (e.g., Cache-Control: max-age=86400 for 1 day)

Title: Verify theme CSS caching and efficient loading
Preconditions: Organization with Coffee theme configured; Browser cache enabled; DevTools Network tab
Steps:

2. Navigate to homepage (clear cache first)
3. Open DevTools > Network > CSS filter
4. Observe theme-related CSS file loading
5. Verify Cache-Control headers

Expected Result: Theme CSS is efficiently loaded and cached: Theme CSS file is minified (smaller file size); First request returns HTTP 200 with Cache-Control; Subsequent pages serve CSS from cache (no re-download); CSS is NOT inlined (unless very small); CSS file size is reasonable (< 100KB); No duplicate CSS files loaded; CSS is loaded early (in <head>) to avoid FOUC (Flash of Unstyled Content); Caching strategy is effective

Title: Verify no FOUC (Flash of Unstyled Content) when loading themed page

Expected Result: No FOUC when loading themed pages: Page loads with Coffee theme colors immediately (no flash of Default theme); No momentary display of unstyled HTML before CSS applies; Theme CSS is loaded in <head> before content renders; Critical CSS may be inlined to prevent FOUC; Page rendering is smooth and professional; User does NOT see "flicker" or color changes during page load; Meets performance and UX best practices

11. Navigate pages in both sessions simultaneously
12. Verify branding remains correct in each session
Expected Result: Concurrent users see correct organization branding: User A session displays Org A branding (logo-a, Coffee theme); User B session displays Org B branding (logo-b, Default theme); No cross-contamination between sessions (User A does NOT see Org B branding); Each session is isolated (correct branding persists); Navigating pages in one session does NOT affect the other; Server correctly serves organization-specific branding based on user context; No caching conflicts between sessions
